title: pterophylla raiateensis
intro: Pterophylla raiateensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the Raiatea custard apple, native to the island of Raiatea in French Polynesia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pterophylla raiateensis include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as extracts and infusions, are often used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
